method_atom: a discretized statistical field model of many strongly-coupled fermionic degrees of freedom interacting with a compact local gauge field, engineered so that a continuous internal symmetry is spontaneously broken and its associated order-parameter scale can be tuned arbitrarily small (via an auxiliary extra dimension), while a residual symmetry is explicitly broken by adding a higher-order self-interaction term; the construction is chosen specifically so that efficient sampling algorithms exist to compute observables in the singular limit where the mass/order parameter vanishes, and the results are validated against the universal predictions of a symmetry-based low-energy effective (perturbation) theory in a finite-volume regime.
object_atom: the low-energy pion sector of two-flavor QCD — the light Goldstone modes of chiral symmetry breaking — represented by two flavors of staggered quarks coupled strongly to U(1) gauge fields, including the axial U(1) anomaly, studied in the ε-regime of chiral perturbation theory.
brief_reason: The transferable part is the strong-coupling lattice field construction with a tunable spontaneously-broken symmetry, explicit residual-symmetry breaking, and algorithm-friendly access to a singular limit checked against an effective theory; the domain-specific part is the QCD pion/quark chiral-dynamics system it models.
